meta.method: Web searches (July 2026) per topic area; concepts an expert would expect beyond the source docs. All tagged source_type=enhancement, curriculum phase 3 (days 15-21).

concepts.name: GraphRAG
concepts.summary: Microsoft's graph-based RAG: LLM extracts an entity knowledge graph + community detection, enabling multi-hop retrieval and global 'summarise the whole corpus' questions flat chunk retrieval can't answer.

concepts.name: Agentic RAG
concepts.summary: A reasoning loop around retrieval: instead of one retrieve-then-generate pass, the agent asks 'do I have enough? is it relevant? should I reformulate and search again?' — the 2026 default for hard queries.

concepts.name: Contextual Retrieval
concepts.summary: Anthropic technique: prepend an LLM-generated situating sentence ('This chunk is from the SLA section of...') to every chunk before embedding — cuts failed retrievals sharply by giving chunks document-level context.

concepts.name: Semantic Chunking
concepts.summary: Split where embedding similarity between adjacent sentences drops (topic shift) instead of at fixed word counts — chunks follow meaning boundaries. Costlier; adopt only when retrieval metrics justify it.

concepts.name: Late Chunking
concepts.summary: Embed the whole document with a long-context embedding model FIRST, then split the token embeddings into chunks — each chunk's vector carries long-range context that independently-embedded chunks miss.

concepts.name: HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings)
concepts.summary: Ask the LLM to write a hypothetical answer to the query, embed THAT, and search with it — answer-shaped text lands closer to real answer passages than question-shaped text does.

concepts.name: ColBERT / Late Interaction
concepts.summary: Middle ground between bi- and cross-encoders: store per-token vectors, compare token-by-token (MaxSim) at query time. Near cross-encoder quality at medium speed; ColBERTv2 is canonical, jina-reranker-v3 ('last but not late') is the 2026 twist.

concepts.name: Learned Sparse Retrieval (SPLADE)
concepts.summary: Neural model outputs weighted keyword expansions (sparse vectors) — BM25's exact-match strengths plus learned synonyms, servable from a standard inverted index. pgvector 0.9 added sparse vector support.

concepts.name: Matryoshka Embeddings (MRL)
concepts.summary: Train embeddings so the first N dims form a valid smaller embedding: truncate 1024→256 dims and lose only ~1-3% recall. Supported by most 2026 models (OpenAI text-3, Gemini Embedding 2, Nomic, Jina v5).

concepts.name: Embedding Quantization (int8 / binary)
concepts.summary: Store vectors as int8 or 1-bit instead of float32: 4-32x less memory at ~1-5% recall cost. Same trade-off idea as LLM weight quantization, applied to the vector store.

concepts.name: MTEB Benchmark
concepts.summary: Massive Text Embedding Benchmark — the standard leaderboard for choosing embedding models. 2026 retrieval leaders include NV-Embed-v2, Gemini Embedding, BGE-M3, Voyage, and mxbai-embed-large.

concepts.name: BGE-M3 and Modern Embedding Models
concepts.summary: BGE-large's successor: one model emitting dense, sparse, AND multi-vector (ColBERT-style) representations, multilingual, 8K-token inputs — hybrid search from a single model instead of separate systems.

concepts.name: KV Cache
concepts.summary: During generation, each token's attention Keys/Values are cached so they aren't recomputed for every new token — the reason long chats consume GPU memory, and the main target of 2026 serving optimisations.

concepts.name: PagedAttention / vLLM
concepts.summary: vLLM manages KV cache like OS virtual memory — fixed-size pages mapped by a page table — cutting the traditional 60-80% KV memory waste to near zero and serving 2-3x more concurrent requests per GPU.

concepts.name: FlashAttention
concepts.summary: IO-aware exact attention kernel that tiles computation to avoid writing the big attention matrix to slow GPU memory — big speed/memory wins; successor kernels (FlashInfer) add block-sparse KV and JIT compilation.

concepts.name: Speculative Decoding
concepts.summary: A small draft model proposes several tokens; the big model verifies them in one parallel pass — accepted tokens come out several-at-a-time, speeding generation without changing output quality.

concepts.name: Continuous Batching
concepts.summary: Server-side batching where finished sequences are evicted and new requests inserted mid-flight — no head-of-line blocking, unlike static batches that wait for the slowest request.

concepts.name: Mixture of Experts (MoE)
concepts.summary: Architecture where a router activates only a few 'expert' sub-networks per token — a model can have huge total parameters but small active compute per token (e.g., Mixtral, DeepSeek family).

concepts.name: Native Function / Tool Calling
concepts.summary: Model APIs now accept JSON-schema tool definitions and return structured tool calls directly — replacing regex-parsed text formats like classic ReAct THOUGHT/ACTION blocks in production agents.

concepts.name: Constrained Decoding / Structured Outputs
concepts.summary: Masking invalid tokens during sampling so output is GUARANTEED to match a JSON schema/grammar — stronger than low temperature + format instructions, which only make compliance likely.

concepts.name: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
concepts.summary: Open standard (Anthropic, Nov 2024; donated to Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation Dec 2025) that standardises how agents discover and call tools/data sources. Near-universal by 2026: 10,000+ public servers, all major vendors. Wraps function calling, doesn't replace it.

concepts.name: Agent Orchestration Frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen)
concepts.summary: 2026 landscape: LangGraph dominates production (graph state machine, checkpointing, human-in-the-loop); CrewAI fastest prototyping; AutoGen/AG2 research multi-agent debate; first-party SDKs when you don't need heavy orchestration.

concepts.name: Multi-Agent Systems / A2A
concepts.summary: Multiple specialised agents coordinating (planner/researcher/coder), with agent-to-agent (A2A) protocols maturing alongside MCP in 2026. Powerful but adds failure modes — single agents still preferred when sufficient.

concepts.name: LoRA / QLoRA
concepts.summary: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning: train small low-rank adapter matrices instead of all weights (10-20x cheaper, 90-95% of full quality); QLoRA does it on a 4-bit-quantized base for single-GPU training.

concepts.name: DPO and Preference Optimization
concepts.summary: Direct Preference Optimization and cousins (IPO, KTO, ORPO, GRPO) replaced the RLHF SFT-then-PPO pipeline as the 2026 default for preference alignment — simpler to run, competitive quality, ~1-5K ranked pairs.

concepts.name: RAG Evaluation (RAGAS)
concepts.summary: Automated RAG metrics along two axes: retrieval (context relevance/precision/recall) and generation (faithfulness, answer correctness) — RAGAS and DeepEval are the standard frameworks; extends the project's keyword-assertion tests.

concepts.name: LLM-as-Judge
concepts.summary: Use a strong LLM to grade another model's answers against criteria/references — standard 2026 eval practice, competitive with specialised detectors, though judge consistency and bias need care (few-shot human anchors help).

concepts.name: Hallucination / Groundedness Detection & Guardrails
concepts.summary: Runtime checks that an answer is supported by retrieved context (groundedness/faithfulness), plus abstention policies ('say I don't know when unsupported') and input/output guardrail filters.

concepts.name: Modern Reranker Landscape
concepts.summary: 2026 upgrades over ms-marco-MiniLM: BGE-Reranker-v2-m3 (open, self-host), Cohere Rerank 4 (API), jina-reranker-v3. Cross-encoder rerankers typically add +5-15 NDCG@10 over vector-only retrieval.

concepts.name: pgvector
concepts.summary: Postgres extension for vector search — 2026's default for most teams: up to ~50M vectors inside the database you already run, no separate system. v0.9 (2026) added sparse vectors and big speed-ups.

concepts.name: Qdrant and Milvus
concepts.summary: Dedicated vector DBs: Qdrant (single binary, fast filtered queries via payload index) for most production RAG; Milvus for billion-scale, Kubernetes-native deployments. LanceDB's niche = edge/desktop/embedded.

concepts.name: IVF and Product Quantization (PQ)
concepts.summary: ANN alternatives to HNSW: IVF clusters vectors and searches only nearby cells; PQ compresses vectors into small codes. IVF-PQ trades a little recall for far less memory at large scale.

concepts.name: Reasoning Models / Test-Time Compute
concepts.summary: Models trained to emit long private 'thinking tokens' before answering (o3/o4-mini, Claude extended thinking, Gemini Deep Think, DeepSeek R1) — trained-in chain of thought; much stronger on math/code/planning, slower and pricier.

concepts.name: Long Context vs RAG
concepts.summary: With 128K-1M+ windows, stuffing docs into context beats RAG for multi-step reasoning over bounded material; RAG still wins for freshness, corpus scale, cost, and citations. 2026 practice: hybrid — retrieve broadly, then reason long-context over it.

concepts.name: Prompt Caching
concepts.summary: Provider-side caching of repeated prompt prefixes (system prompt, tool definitions, documents) so they're not reprocessed each call — large cost/latency savings; complements semantic answer caching.

concepts.name: Free-Threaded Python (GIL Removal)
concepts.summary: PEP 703: Python 3.13+ ships an optional free-threaded build with no GIL, making threads genuinely parallel for CPU work — gradually weakening the classic 'threads for I/O, processes for CPU' rule.

source_updates.src-013: 2026 update: DPO-family methods (DPO/IPO/KTO/ORPO/GRPO) have largely replaced the classic RLHF PPO pipeline as the default preference-alignment approach.
source_updates.src-016: 2026 update: BGE-large-en-v1.5 is no longer the open-source leader — BGE-M3, NV-Embed-v2, Gemini Embedding, Voyage and mxbai-embed-large top MTEB retrieval in 2026.
source_updates.src-021: 2026 update: production RAG has evolved toward agentic RAG (retrieval in a reasoning loop), GraphRAG for multi-hop questions, and hybrid long-context+RAG designs.
source_updates.src-027: 2026 update: LanceDB's recognised niche is edge/desktop/embedded; pgvector is the common default (≤50M vectors), Qdrant/Milvus for dedicated or billion-scale serving.
source_updates.src-029: 2026 update: HNSW remains standard, but IVF-PQ variants and pgvector 0.9's improved IVFFlat are common at scale for memory reasons.
source_updates.src-042: 2026 update: ms-marco-MiniLM is dated — BGE-Reranker-v2-m3, Cohere Rerank 4, and jina-reranker-v3 are current; late-interaction (ColBERT) sits between bi- and cross-encoders.
source_updates.src-051: 2026 update: text-parsed ReAct (THOUGHT/ACTION via regex) is legacy in production — native function calling APIs and MCP-standardised tools are the norm; the reason-act loop concept itself lives on.
source_updates.src-052: 2026 update: tool definitions are being standardised by MCP (Model Context Protocol), now under the Linux Foundation with support from all major AI vendors.
source_updates.src-054: 2026 update: constrained decoding / structured-output APIs guarantee schema-valid JSON, replacing 'low temperature + format rules + regex' reliability tricks.
source_updates.src-055: 2026 update: chain-of-thought has been trained into dedicated reasoning models (o3, Claude extended thinking, DeepSeek R1) that spend test-time compute on private thinking tokens.
source_updates.src-065: 2026 update: Python 3.13+ offers a free-threaded (no-GIL) build (PEP 703), so the GIL constraint is no longer absolute on modern Python.
source_updates.src-008: 2026 update: frontier context windows reach 1M+ tokens, fueling the 'long context vs RAG' debate — long context wins for multi-step reasoning over bounded docs, RAG for scale and freshness.
